remember: Students will be able to recall the composition of the atmosphere, including the different gases and their proportions.
Students will be able to identify the various layers of the atmosphere and their characteristics.
understand: Students will be able to explain the structure of the atmosphere, including the troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere, and exosphere.
Students will be able to describe the importance of the atmosphere in regulating the Earth's climate and weather patterns.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of the atmosphere to understand the impact of human activities on the environment.
Students will be able to use the concept of atmospheric layers to explain various weather phenomena.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the atmosphere and other components of the Earth's system, including the hydrosphere and lithosphere.
Students will be able to evaluate the effects of atmospheric changes on the environment and human societies.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the composition and structure of the atmosphere.
To identify the different layers of the atmosphere and their characteristics, as outlined in the NCERT textbook.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests the ability to describe the composition and structure of the atmosphere, including the different layers and their characteristics.
The board exam assesses the understanding of the importance of the atmosphere in regulating the Earth's climate and weather patterns.
